Goal: Semi-automated documentation of existing Lean code inside the same planning/proofbook tool.
Motivation: The Bestandscode cannot be fully normalized at once, but every module, declaration family, object, proof bridge, and consumer path can be documented with the same YAML/proof-dossier infrastructure so later interpretation is minimized.
Vision: Existing Lean-code documentation should grow into a reviewable object universe: each missing object/quantity becomes a dossier-backed node that can be inspected by the relevant expert without reading the full repository.
Raw data / tools: repo_inventory/raw_export modules.json, import_edges.json, symbol_reference_summary.json, dependency_order, mainline forensics, object register, proof dossiers, implementation scratchpad.
Operational scope: Secondary workflow: document existing code and its true consumers while Phase 1.x cleans the public API. It does not authorize refactors or deletion by itself.
Not-before gate: After Phase 1.1.1.1.4 declaration consumption classification and initial proof-dossier infrastructure are closed; deeper execution after Phase 1.1.5 import firewall is green.
Derived-only boundary: Documentation may classify provenance and consumers; it may not turn graph metadata into CNNA-generative assumptions.
Plan action: Add Phase 19.1 and object LEG16; use the generated Lean module graph to prevent forgotten code/documentation gaps. V1.12 adds LEG36 so existing-code documentation also records semantic consolidation/no-merge candidates before new substantive code.

Goal: Derivation-graph geometry of the formalized theory.
Motivation: The repository already contains graph-theoretic data: import graph, dominators, layer decomposition, source-sink signal paths, and robust semantic mainline. These can later expose bottlenecks, weak derivation edges, and discipline clusters.
Vision: A later graph/database layer can provide semantic zoom from pillars to phases, modules, declarations, objects, proof dossiers, and theorem sites; the graph remains a diagnostic/evidence map, not a generator.
Raw data / tools: import_edges.json, dominator analysis, layer decomposition, source-to-sink paths, module group graph, architectural core summary, mainline forensics.
Operational scope: Secondary research/diagnostic layer only. Use it to understand theory organization and proof pressure after core derivations are stable.
Not-before gate: After the neutral API, generator governance, object proof dossiers, and main operational arithmetic/DtN paths have stabilized; not before active Phase 1.x cleanup.
Derived-only boundary: Graph geometry can suggest audits and consistency checks but cannot supply mathematical axioms, stochasticity, open-system behavior, or physical interpretation.
Plan action: Add Phase 19.2 and object LEG17; render/import future graph metrics such as dominators, cuts, signal-path depth, cluster boundaries, and optional Cheeger-style diagnostics as comparison metadata.

Goal: Formal emergence-path forensics for open-system dynamics inside the derivation network.
Motivation: Later, once Pillar A/C objects are derived, the proof/module graph may help locate where closed finite subsystem assumptions become open effective dynamics through boundary/complement/regime handoffs.
Vision: When A/C open-system paths exist, graph forensics can show where regime, complement, boundary, and handoff structures make openness visible, but the dynamics must still be derived in Lean-facing CNNA objects.
Raw data / tools: A-regime profiles, complement-family profiles, DtN/Schur handoff modules, C/OQS consumer path, source-sink paths, dominator/cut analysis, object proof dossiers.
Operational scope: Post-core analysis of the formalization topology; not a replacement for deriving open-system behavior inside CNNA.
Not-before gate: After A regime/complement path, A->C handoff, and C OQS consumer roadmaps have enough checked objects to analyze. Treat as bonus/secondary until then.
Derived-only boundary: Emergence claims must still be proved from CNNA objects; graph forensics only localize proof paths, bottlenecks, and structural analogies.
Plan action: Add Phase 19.3 and object LEG18; keep as black/secondary target until the relevant A/C derivations exist.

Goal: Reflective comparison between CNNA object-structure and formalization-structure.
Motivation: If the formal derivation graph later exhibits structural features resembling CNNA objects - layering, boundary cuts, operators on graphs, signal flow, modular clusters - this should be studied as a meta-level observation, not as a premise.
Vision: The tool may be read as a meta-level interface analogue: one active cursor/bright object is examined against the large Lean-code complement; this is an operational review metaphor, not a CNNA theorem.
Raw data / tools: discipline subgraphs for Dirichlet/DtN/Schur, prenumerical/ToC/Substrate, Topology/Algebra/CD; module clusters; graph cuts; source-sink paths; proof-dossier links.
Operational scope: Far-target meta-analysis and visualization after mathematical closure. It may produce diagnostics, figures, and research notes, not new CNNA base assumptions.
Not-before gate: Only after core derived objects, public API, and consumer handoffs are stable enough that graph measurements are meaningful.
Derived-only boundary: Strictly comparison/diagnostic. Any apparent self-similarity between theory and formalization remains an observation unless separately formalized and proved.
Plan action: Add Phase 19.4 and object LEG19; keep outside the active implementation path.

Motivation: The IdealThread/full-cut extrema may later illuminate how one-dimensional recursive routes sit inside full boundary/operator geometry and how open-system behavior emerges from complement/interface structure.
Vision: Future string-analogy diagnostics may use derivation geometry as a comparison map around the thread/full-boundary extrema, only after the underlying route is derived and reviewed.
Raw data / tools: IdealThread/CutSpec.full profiles; import graph; derivation graph; Schur/DtN route audit; proof dossiers; source-sink paths and dominators.
Operational scope: Secondary diagnostic/comparison study only, after the underlying CNNA objects are code-derived and public-final.
Not-before gate: After S07, S08, BR06, BR07, S05/S06, and the relevant arithmetic/number-theory proof dossiers are closed.
Derived-only boundary: The diagnostic may compare formalization geometry to CNNA objects but cannot feed back as a generative axiom, formula, or shortcut.
Plan action: Keep as Phase 19.5 side-road; do not block active Phase 1 cleanup or Phase 3/4 neutralization.

Goal: Database-ready CNNA Explorer and review/documentation structure
Motivation: A database-backed representation is likely the most effective path for querying, analyzing, visualizing, sharing, online display, interactive exploration, and discipline-separated expert review of the CNNA object world.
Vision: Long-term vision: a custom CNNA Explorer that starts as a dark, SQL/YAML-driven, visual research interface over the Single-YAML-Master mirror and can gradually grow into a Universe Explorer. The user-facing surface should support Pillar hubs, graph navigation, right-side inspectors, filters by context/theme/phase/object/module/status/edge semantics, history timelines, multilingual descriptions, hypothesis tracking and later lab views. The explorer remains generated-secondary: Lean code is the primary truth system, YAML is the curated semantic mirror, SQL is a read-only presentation mirror.
Raw data / tools: Single YAML master, generated TSV/JSON/DOT/PDF views, phase/object/proof-dossier records, module inventory, status legends, local theory context ledger, end-theory comparison ledger, future relational or graph database export.; future GitHub Actions artifacts; snapshot_manifest.json; Webhosting-local MySQL importer; SQL dumps/local archives
Operational scope: Main side objective Phase 20 plus dedicated CNNA Explorer main block Phase 21. Phase 21.0 must close the completeness/coherence/refactor/source-inventory audit before Explorer schema, SQL importer or frontend implementation are treated as stable.
Not-before gate: Principles apply immediately. Technical database export follows after YAML schema and generated views are stable enough for round-trip-safe identifiers and review links. Public deployment additionally requires backup/rollback evidence, read-only API tests and validated staging/current import.
Derived-only boundary: The database is a documentation/review/query layer. It may expose, analyze, and visualize Lean/YAML-derived metadata, but it cannot supply CNNA-generative axioms, formulas, physics, stochasticity, or established-theory inputs.
Plan action: P1.37 sharpens Phase 21 with 21.2.1-21.2.3: GitHub backup-gated PR automation, CI build/snapshot artifacts, and Webhosting-local MySQL staging/import/swap/rollback. The database remains read-only/generated-secondary and cannot replace Lean/YAML truth.

Goal: Geometric-algebra comparison map for later CNNA-derived algebraic and spacetime structures
Motivation: Quaternion/Pauli/Clifford/geometric-algebra literature provides compact target profiles for rotations, bivectors, pseudoscalars, Clifford signatures and possible spacetime/conformal comparison layers.
Vision: After CNNA derives relevant CD, boundary-algebra, AQFT/spacetime or side-comparison outputs, the tool can generate a review map showing which GA targets are matched, failed or not yet meaningful.
Raw data / tools: Horn 0709.2238v1, Hestenes references, mathlib Quaternion/CliffordAlgebra docs, Wieser/Song Lean GA formalization, proof dossiers GA0-GA6.
Operational scope: Long-term comparison/review map only; no foundation, no phase release, no Lean generator input.
Not-before gate: After GA0-GA6 predecessor phases expose CNNA-derived candidate data; especially CD reintegration and Pillar-B boundary/local-net phases.
Derived-only boundary: GA references remain blue/reference material. A target match is evidence of compatibility, not a source of truth; a mismatch must be recorded rather than patched at the consumer.
Plan action: V1.25 adds phases 1.4.9.*, 13.2.1, 13.6.1 and 18.5 plus objects GA0-GA6.
